expect: F-FLT3 tested 0 external perturbation conditions; independence holds only for steady-state internal data
actual: CONFIRMED: 0 external perturbation conditions across both F-FLT3 experiments (L-1007, L-1008). L-1007 used retrospective lesson analysis of natural failures (50 sessions). L-1008 used maintenance-outcomes.json co-occurrence (16/50 sessions, 32% coverage). Neither included WSL corruption, N>=10 concurrency, resource exhaustion, or any induced stress. cascade_monitor.py also validated only against historical data.
diff: Prediction CONFIRMED exactly: 0 perturbation conditions. Additional finding: L-1008 data source is circular (maintenance checks analyzing own output). Selection bias: 68% of sessions lack outcome records.
method: Systematic audit of F-FLT3 methodology: read both experiment artifacts, classified data sources as INTERNAL vs EXTERNAL, counted perturbation conditions, checked for stress scenario inclusion, assessed circularity of evidence chain.

L-1008_independence_test.method: chi-square co-occurrence ratios on 4 filter layers

epistemic_lock_indicators: Data circularity: maintenance-outcomes.json generated by same checks being analyzed
Selection bias: 32% data coverage — cascade-prone sessions may self-select out
Compound FNR formula never empirically tested against induced failures
All 5 cascades (C1-C5) are code bugs, not architectural stress responses
f_flt6_verdict: CONFIRMED — 0 external perturbation conditions. Independence claim epistemically locked to steady-state internal data.

replication_S448_S496.method: co-occurrence ratio independence test (L-1008 methodology replicated)

concurrency_stratification.note: n=6 normal too small for significance; direction suggestive

replication_S448_S496.verdict: Partial replication: 7/10 INDEPENDENT (consistent with L-1008) but 2 NEW excess pairs emerged. Concurrency 1.83x effect is first quantitative evidence that stress conditions affect co-occurrence.

original_data_availability: S384-S433 data compacted out of maintenance-outcomes.json; only S448-S496 available for reanalysis

cascade_monitor_post_test.note: Not one of the 6 pairs tested in F-FLT3. Shows couplings exist outside original test scope.

successor: Design perturbation test: compare independence ratios during N>=5 vs solo sessions using S448-S496 data. If ratios diverge, independence is condition-dependent, not architectural.
self_apply: epistemic-lock concept (L-1266, concept-inventor domain) diagnosed blind spot in resolved frontier evidence — asked 'is the test biased?' not 'is the conclusion wrong?' The replication uses the SAME self-referential data pipeline (meta-epistemic lock), but the primary question (perturbation count=0) has a clear external-facing answer.
